summary: A single test in the PubSub suite timed out waiting to receive a channel message, causing the CI test target to fail.
root_cause: The test 'PubSub [It] should ChannelMessage' in pubsub_test.go timed out while waiting for a message on a channel (the test expected a receive but none arrived within the timeout).

evidence.reason: Shows the failing test name and that it failed.
Identifies the test file and line associated with the failure.
Indicates the test failed due to a timeout (did not observe expected event in time).

evidence.quote:
Expected
        <<-chan *redis.Message | len:0, cap:10>: 0xc0012d96c0
    to receive something.
evidence.reason: The assertion that something should have been received from the channel failed (no message arrived).

evidence.quote:
Ran 1224 of 1274 Specs in 179.248 seconds
FAIL! -- 1223 Passed | 1 Failed
evidence.reason: Summarizes that one test failed among many, confirming this was the cause of the overall test suite failure.
The failing test caused the CI make target to exit with an error.

suggested_fix: Run the failing test locally and inspect pubsub_test.go around lines 645–661 to determine why no message was received; ensure the publisher path in the test actually publishes before the receive or extend the test timeout / make the test more robust (e.g., retry or synchronize publisher and subscriber). Re-run CI after addressing timing or synchronization issues.
